name: Banner
description: A banner is a prominent message with one or two actions, shown at the top of a region. It surfaces something important but non-blocking — vehicles due for service, a sync issue, a new feature — and stays until the operator acts.
keyRule: Use a banner for a persistent, moderate-priority message that needs acknowledgement. For transient confirmation use a snackbar; for a blocking decision use a dialog.

variants.name: Informational
variants.whenToUse: Neutral notice (a new export format, a tip).

variants.name: Error
variants.whenToUse: A problem affecting the data on screen (sync failed).

anatomy.name: Container
anatomy.description: spans the region width, sits at the top.

anatomy.name: Actions
anatomy.description: one or two text buttons (e.g. Dismiss + Review).

behaviors.name: Persistence
behaviors.description: A banner stays until dismissed or resolved — unlike a snackbar, it doesn't auto-hide.

behaviors.name: Placement
behaviors.description: Anchor it at the top of the relevant region (page or section), above the content it concerns.

behaviors.name: One at a time
behaviors.description: Show a single banner per region; queue or combine rather than stacking several.

states.treatment: Tinted background per severity; message + actions.

specs.value: success/warning/error/info container colours; text meets AA

props.name: severity
props.description: Controls tint and the live-region politeness (error is assertive).

doThis: Match the tint to severity and keep text legible.
dontDoThis: Don't use a banner for transient confirmations (use a snackbar).
Don't use error styling for non-errors.
accessibility: Expose the banner via an appropriate live region (assertive for errors, polite for info) so it's announced.
Actions are real buttons with accessible names.
Severity is conveyed by icon + text, not colour alone, and tints meet AA contrast.

related.name: Dialog
related.why: When the operator must decide before continuing
